item.title: Dennis Cometti, legendary sports commentator, dies aged 76
item.description: Dennis Cometti — who called a range of sports for the ABC, Seven and Nine and was lauded for his football commentary over 50-plus years in the industry — is remembered as "the best of his kind" after his death aged 76.

item.title: Albanese government did not act on advice to halt salmon farming at Macquarie Harbour
item.description: Former environment minister Tanya Plibersek was advised by her department to revoke salmon farming approvals in Tasmania's Macquarie Harbour amid concerns about the industry's impact on the endangered Maugean skate, FOI documents show.

item.title: Adelaide Fringe ticket sales 'drop off a cliff' because of fizzer rain forecast
item.description: A dire weather forecast for last weekend in Adelaide combine with ongoing cost of living pressures to deliver a huge drop-off in Adelaide Fringe ticket sales, organisers and artists say.

item.title: Netball teams ousted from league due to lack of footballers sparks equal opportunity complaint
item.description: Victoria's Equal Opportunity and Human Rights Commission has been contacted after a number of junior netball teams were axed from the Ballarat Football Netball league due to a lack of footballers.

item.title: Body found during search for tourist missing from Gold Coast beach
item.description: Police say emergency services were called after reports a 20-year-old man, who is believed to be a tourist, was seen struggling in the water off Main Beach.

item.title: Police shoot allegedly armed man on Sunshine Coast after car crash
item.description: Police say a man became involved in a confrontation with officers on Nambour Connection Road in Woombye after earlier crashing his car 10 kilometres away.

item.title: Children reported to Queensland Health over 'unlicensed' cupcake stand
item.description: Kelly Stothar says she was flabbergasted when she received a letter from Brisbane City Council threatening her with fines over her children's cupcake stand.

item.title: Man arrested in missing persons case of Brendan Breen, football hero's brother
item.description: Police have made an arrest in relation to the long-running missing persons case of the fraudster brother of a former St Kilda footballer.

item.title: Legendary sports broadcaster Dennis Cometti has died aged 76, his family has confirmed.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Cloud Carrier proposes gas-fired power station to power Moss Vale AI data centre
item.description: A small town in the NSW Southern Highlands is watching the AI revolution take shape in its backyard, as plans emerge to build one of the state's largest gas-fired power stations to run a data-processing complex.

item.title: Australian crisis teams sent to Middle East as Iran war continues
item.description: The federal government is sending six crisis response teams to the Middle East to help thousands of Australians stranded in the region, while the foreign minister continues to brush off questions about whether the US and Israel's strikes on Iran were legal.

item.title: Middle East conflict hits meat exports, pushes up costs for farmers
item.description: Exports have been halted and shipping containers filled with Australian meat are stranded at sea as the Middle East conflict takes a toll on trade.

item.title: ARN Media's $200m Kyle and Jackie O experiment has failed
item.description: The network's mammoth deal banked on the fact that people in Melbourne would take to the hosts just as Sydneysiders did.

item.title: 'Centimetre perfect': Revisiting Dennis Cometti's best one-liners as AFL community mourns commentary icon
item.description: As the AFL community mourns the death of one of its most iconic voices, here are some of the most memorable Dennis Cometti one-liners.

item.title: Tim Picton's accused killer Brodie Jake Dewar released on bail
item.description: Brodie Jake Dewar, 20, who allegedly killed Labor identity Tim Picton after striking him and causing him to fall over, is granted bail by a Perth judge.

item.title: Your photos of the blood moon from around Australia
item.description: Stargazers across the country have shared photos of the blood moon total lunar eclipse, from the city to the beach and regional backyards — here's a look.

item.title: Driver captures unfolding police incident
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police 'missed opportunities' before Kelly Wilkinson murder, inquest hears
item.description: An inquest hears there were flaws in the police response before Gold Coast mother-of-three Kelly Wilkinson was murdered by her estranged husband in 2021.

item.title: Petrol prices surge across Australia, long queues at fuel stations amid Middle East war
item.description: Petrol retailers across the nation are being accused of "unconscionable" behaviour by preying on people's anxieties as fuel prices surge in the midst of the Middle East war.

item.title: Ecuador launches joint anti-drug operations with United States forces
item.description: Ecuador announces joint operations with the United States to combat drug trafficking, which has caused an explosion of violence in the South American country.

item.title: Cost of new Melbourne F1 Grand Prix pit building soars due to asbestos work
item.description: The planned rebuild of a pit lane at Albert Park has blown out by millions of dollars, with Victorian taxpayers to foot the bill.

item.title: Fuel price nears $4 per litre in Arnhem Land, with further increases expected due to war in Middle East
item.description: Remote communities are paying nearly $4 per litre for diesel and NT residents are claiming the price has jumped unfairly this week.

item.title: How airlines determine if it is safe to fly from conflict zones
item.description: A flight from Dubai to Australia has taken off, becoming the first commercial plane to carry Australians home since the Middle East conflict began.

item.title: Why do some of us vividly remember dreams and others say they 'don't dream'?
item.description: Some people are naturally better at recalling their dreams. Others worry they're not getting quality sleep if they can't remember their dreams. Here's the science.

item.title: Dennis Cometti speaks to the ABC in 1984
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dennis Cometti's commentary made him a legend, but dedication off the mic defined him
item.description: Dennis Cometti was known to viewers as a wordsmith who rattled off rejoinders with ease, but behind the scenes he worked harder than anyone to carve out a place in Australia's sporting landscape.

item.title: Two men charged after 120kg drug bust at Euston on Victoria-NSW border
item.description: Police say 120kg of pseudoephedrine found concealed in a shipping container would have been made into illicit drugs.

item.title: Mosman Park murder family friend speaks of ‘harrowing’ reality raising child with severe autism
item.description: Simon Lewis knew the Clune family, who died in an apparent murder-suicide last month, and says his own experience with his severely autistic daughter means he can relate "to what they were going through".

item.title: Former teacher Tayla Lee Brailey pleads guilty to sexual abuse of students
item.description: A woman who once taught at a Sydney school pleads guilty to the sexual abuse of two students.

item.title: Bruce McAvaney mourns Dennis Cometti, hailing fellow AFL commentator his 'ultimate teammate'
item.description: Bruce McAvaney says he has "lost something truly precious" with the death of long-time AFL commentary partner Dennis Cometti.

item.title: Islamic community group Taha Humanity Association's grant cancelled amid 'social cohesion concerns'
item.description: The federal government has cancelled a grant for an Islamic community association in Melbourne citing "social cohesion concerns" after claims surfaced the group was mourning the death of Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Police officer at Tingalpa fatal shooting suffered graze to ear from 'projectile'
item.description: Police were called for a welfare check in an east Brisbane suburb on Tuesday, where a man allegedly holding a knife was shot dead.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Attorney-general pays taxpayer back $8,000 for family flights
item.description: Figures from the expenses watchdog confirm Attorney-General Michelle Rowland has repaid about $8,000 spent on flights for family travel from Perth to Sydney.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands and Jackie 'O' Henderson's former content director Craig Bruce tells of contract 'insanity'
item.description: Craig Bruce helped elevate Kyle Sandilands and Jackie O. He's watched on as the pair have fallen apart underneath the weight of the richest deal in Australian media.

item.title: Mark Carney warns Australia and Canada face 'subordination' by global superpowers
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney calls for middle powers to use their sovereignty in a Sydney speech echoing his Davos intervention.

item.title: Emotional scenes as first commercial flight out of Middle East since war began lands in Sydney
item.description: Emirates flight EK414 from Dubai touched down safely with about 200 Australians on board, after departing Dubai in the early hours of the morning.

item.title: Albanese supported the 'rules-based order' six months ago
item.description: Bipartisan support for the "global rules-based order" has all but disappeared in Australia.

item.title: Tasmanian nurses say lower wages, understaffing a barrier to returning home
item.description: Tasmania needs more nurses, but lower wages and fewer opportunities for career growth remain barriers for enticing health workers to return to their home state.

item.title: Seasonal produce to make the most of in March
item.description: From apples to avocados, these are the early autumn fruit and veggies to look for.

item.title: Car buyers left tens of thousands out of pocket as platform Carconnect enters administration
item.description: Online car-buying platform Carconnect has entered voluntary administration, leaving questions about how its customers will collect their vehicles, and whether they will get their money back.

item.title: Real attack or video game? Misinformation and war in the AI age
item.description: Is it real or is it AI? Misinformation seen by millions is spreading online in the wake of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Canada 'unlikely' to join AUKUS despite previous enthusiasm for pact
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney and senior Canadian administration figures are in Canberra with defence and security expected to dominate the discussions there.

item.title: Conservative party New Zealand First pushes to make English an official language
item.description: New Zealand might be full of English speakers, but some politicians are pushing for laws to protect the language.

item.title: Australian regulator delays ban on plumbing parts containing lead by two years
item.description: The Australian Building Codes Board delays a ban on plumbing products containing lead by two more years, despite backlash from the industry.

item.title: Hopes for lower melanoma risk as study finds number of moles on children's bodies halved in 25 years
item.description: The study — which surveyed almost 4,000 south-east Queensland children — found average mole count plummeted from 87 in 1992 to 46 in 2016 — a 47 per cent reduction.

item.title: Tasmanian salmon farming emergency licence for antibiotic florfenicol revoked over 'unacceptable risk'
item.description: The use of florfenicol in Tasmania's salmon industry has been banned as the country's veterinary medicine regulator suspends an emergency licence for the antibiotic.

item.title: Scientists want help to solve mystery after tagging 10,000 bogong moths
item.description: The project's success largely relies on citizen scientists spotting a distinct white dot on the wings of moths.

item.title: New Zealand posts nine-wicket win over South Africa to reach men's T20 World Cup final
item.description: A 33-ball century to Finn Allen helps New Zealand to a convincing nine-wicket win over South Africa in the first men's T20 World Cup semifinal.

item.title: Why is Donald Trump threatening to end all US trade with Spain?
item.description: Spain's Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez has doubled down on his criticism of the military actions of the US and Israel in Iran, just a day after Donald Trump threatened to end all trade with Spain.

item.title: Australian artists warn government to 'hold its nerve' on AI copyright
item.description: Artists and copyright advocates say big tech is again pushing to loosen Australia's copyright rules as artificial intelligence companies expand.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: What time do the Matildas play? How to watch and follow Australia vs Iran
item.description: After winning their opening Women's Asian Cup game, the Matildas now face Iran on the Gold Coast. Here's everything you need to know about how to follow the game.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: From glory to rebuilding, the challenges facing China's Steel Roses in Australia
item.description: Once a global powerhouse, China's women's national team is navigating the challenges of modern football, from rising competition abroad to resource gaps at home.

item.title: UK police reportedly arrest MP's partner on suspicion of spying for China
item.description: Three men have been arrested by British police and are being held in custody on suspicion of spying for China.

item.title: Ex-Socceroos coach Graham Arnold stranded in UAE as war impacts Iraq's World Cup hopes
item.description: Iraq's World Cup qualification campaign is being affected by the Middle East war, with coach Graham Arnold stranded in United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Why Iran's most powerful ally is not coming to its aid
item.description: China has condemned the Israel-US war with Iran, but it could benefit Beijing's strategic goals.

item.title: Man charged with manslaughter over death of Jarrad Antonovich in ayahuasca and kambo ceremony
item.description: Jarrad Antonovich died from a perforated oesophagus after allegedly participating in a ceremony involving South American hallucinogen ayahuasca and the frog poison, kambo.

item.title: Foreign workers recruited at Mount Isa Mines as Glencore says domestic staff in short supply
item.description: Mining giant Glencore will employ almost 30 international workers at Mount Isa Mines amid criticism the company is overlooking domestic workers.

item.title: How Dennis Cometti provided the soundtrack to a generation of AFL football
item.description: The rich, sonorous voice of iconic sports commentator Dennis Cometti, who has died at the age of 76, will be the soundtrack to a generation of kick-to-kick in the parks and backyards of Australia.

item.title: Search resumes for 18-year-old surf lifesaver off Buddina Beach on Sunshine Coast
item.description: A large-scale search for an 18-year-old lifesaver missing in rough ocean conditions has continued into a second day.

item.title: WA's biggest dairy farm moves to off-grid solar to tackle steep power costs
item.description: Facing rising electricity costs and repeated outages, WA's biggest dairy farm is investing $650,000 in a hybrid solar and battery system to keep irrigation running and milk flowing as the number of dairy farms in the state continues to decline.

item.title: Former Jetstar pilot Greg Lynn loses bail bid as he awaits retrial for alleged murder of Carol Clay
item.description: The 59-year-old has lost a bid for bail made in the wake of a successful appeal against his conviction for the murder of elderly camper Carol Clay.

item.title: When the 'renovator's delight' is the only house you can afford, here's what to look for
item.description: In the market for a ready-to-renovate property? Here are some tips to help you uncover a gem, from Grand Designs Transformations host Anthony Burke — and a couple who just did it with little DIY experience.

item.title: Limited resources to tackle animal abuse and neglect in the Kimberley
item.description: Animal welfare advocates in the Kimberley say more needs to be done to stop animal abuse and neglect.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Mackenzie Arnold fit as Montemurro teases changes against Iran
item.description: Matildas goalkeeper Mackenzie Arnold is fit for tonight's Women's Asian Cup game against Iran on the Gold Coast, with coach Joe Montemurro conceding he will have to rotate his squad.

item.title: What to expect in 2026 AFL season as Lions chase third straight premiership
item.description: As the AFL premiership gets underway on Thursday, there will be 17 teams furiously trying to stop the Brisbane Lions winning a third straight flag.

item.title: Coolgardie RSL enjoying revival by appealing to wider community
item.description: As many regional RSL clubs across Western Australia struggle to remain relevant, one sub-branch in a small Goldfields town has shown it is possible to thrive.

item.title: SA Police investigating death of nine-month-old baby boy in Adelaide's northern suburbs
item.description: Police are investigating after the death of a nine-month-old baby boy at a house in Adelaide's northern suburbs.

item.title: Oscar Piastri says McLaren is not favourite to win F1 Australian Grand Prix
item.description: With a brand new car to wrap his head around, Oscar Piastri puts the disappointment of 2025 behind him as Formula 1's new season begins in Melbourne.

item.title: Laramba residents' court battle with NT government over uranium levels in drinking water hits roadblock
item.description: Residents from the isolated community of Laramba in Central Australia are suing the NT government over claims it failed to address the high levels of uranium in their drinking water.

item.title: Star's former CEO and lawyer broke Corporations Act, Chinese money laundering case judgement finds
item.description: Star casino's former top boss and its legal counsel both breached their director's duties and "failed" to heed warnings about money laundering by Chinese criminal syndicates, the Federal Court in Sydney has found.

item.title: Inside the three-way deal that could see Australians stranded in Gulf get home
item.description: The mammoth task of getting stranded Australians out of the Middle East is part of a fascinating story of backchannel diplomacy between Gulf countries and Iran that has implications for the likely breakout of broader conflict in the region.

item.title: Warning fracking on Tanumbirini Station could damage hot springs
item.description: Fears hot springs in the remote NT could be damaged by fracking on a cattle station prompt water scientists and Indigenous custodians to call on the territory government to step in and reject an application by Santos to drill in the area.

item.title: What to watch in March, from manosphere influencers to dinosaur documentaries
item.description: This month sees Louis Theroux back on the tools to dive into the manosphere, Nicole Kidman team up with Jamie Lee Curtis for a new crime show and much more.

item.title: Coroner finds Chinese students found huddled near High Country hut died of hypothermia
item.description: A coroner has determined that two Chinese students found dead while hiking in the High Country died from hypothermia.

item.title: Alleged gunman faces court in Maroochydore after threatening police at Woombye
item.description: A 32-year-old man who was shot by police faces court on multiple charges, including a weapons charge relating to a revolver.

item.title: ETU NSW president Glen Potter charged after police allegedly seized guns and cash
item.description: Police have charged Eletrical Trades Union NSW president Glen Potter after allegedly finding guns and $120,000 inside his home.

item.title: Kelly Wilkinson murder inquest abruptly halted after stunning new allegations
item.description: An inquest into the brutal 2021 murder of Kelly Wilkinson has been unexpectedly adjourned after fresh allegations about the Gold Coast mother-of-three's last interactions with police.

item.title: Trailblazing leader of First Nations peak bodies Pat Turner retires
item.description: Indigenous trailblazer Pat Turner has announced her retirement after a "tireless" career dedicated to championing the rights of First Nations people.

item.title: Woman arrested and charged with criminal neglect after baby boy's death
item.description: A woman has been arrested and charged with criminal neglect after the death of a nine-month-old baby boy, police say.

item.title: AI-generated film at Tropfest 2026 sparks filmmaker backlash
item.description: A completely AI-generated film was selected among 16 finalists out of 700 entries at the recently returned short film festival.

item.title: Qld man avoids jail after causing crash at 'notorious' South Australian intersection
item.description: A Queensland man has avoided jail for causing a serious crash at an Adelaide freeway intersection after ignoring "extensive signage".

item.title: Monash IVF to pay financial settlement to families caught up in embryo bungles
item.description: Fertility giant Monash IVF has agreed to pay financial settlements to families involved in two major bungles that saw two women transferred the wrong embryo.

item.title: Space start-up Equatorial Launch Australia goes into liquidation, owing NT government $5m
item.description: A space start-up once billed as the future of Australia's rocket launch industry has collapsed into liquidation, still owing the Northern Territory government $5 million.

item.title: Man sentenced to 20 years' jail for killing 'gentle, kind' 16-year-old Narromine boy
item.description: Court hears Anthony Facchi was on bail when he walked into a house uninvited and fatally stabbed a 16-year-old boy he had never met.

item.title: Fraud charges against Sunraysia wine grapegrowers Littore brothers dropped
item.description: The Littore brothers have had their charges over allegedly conspiring to defraud Rabobank Australia of more than $19 million dropped in the County Court of Victoria.

item.title: China keeps defence spending high in drive to modernise
item.description: China will boost defence spending by 7 per cent in 2026, still outpacing wider economic growth targets at a time of growing regional tension, including over Taiwan.

item.title: Tim Wilson does his best Billy Joel while Angus Taylor gets politicking
item.description: This week in Canberra, Tim Wilson did his best Billy Joel impression, the opposition leader went hard on the government over the cost-of-living crisis and the Canadian prime minister popped into parliament.

item.title: F1 Australian Grand Prix: Aston Martin drivers 'risk permanent nerve damage' to finish race
item.description: Aston Martin will almost certainly not finish this weekend's Formula 1 Australian Grand Prix, if it even starts at all, over fears the vibration of the car could permanently injure its drivers.

item.title: Peter Damjanovic jailed for brutal murder of partner Ms Woodley at her Bedford home
item.description: A man who bludgeoned his partner to death with a towel rail and shower head in the bathroom of her home is jailed for a minimum of 21 years.

item.title: Research finds Karenia cristata algae blamed for SA algal bloom effects had been around for a decade
item.description: New research has found that the dominant species of algae killing marine life along South Australia's coastline has been present in the state's waters for at least a decade.

item.title: Fatal hit-run driver Jake Danby pleads guilty to speeding, unlicensed driving charges in Darwin Local Court
item.description: A driver who hit two pedestrians in Darwin, one of whom died, fled the scene and then boasted about the crash to friends has been allowed to keep his driver’s licence.

item.title: Petford Training Farm founder Geoff Guest found guilty of child sexual abuse
item.description: A jury returns majority guilty verdicts in the trial of Geoff Guest, 99, who has an Order of Australia Medal for his youth work.

item.title: 'It wasn't a great 30 minutes': Blues react to horror quarter in Swans loss
item.description: Carlton is left to reflect on Sydney's dominant third quarter, with the Swans pouring on 75 points as they ran away to complete a convincing victory at the SCG.

item.title: Eels served 'big schooner of reality' after 48-point loss to Storm
item.description: After conceding 52 unanswered points, Parramatta is left shocked by the Storm's performance as round one of the NRL continued in Melbourne.

item.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Matildas beat Iran, but trail in race for Asian Cup group top spot
item.description: The Matildas beat Iran at Robina to qualify for the knockout stages of the Women's Asian Cup — but four goals are not enough to take them top of their group.

item.title: Astrid Linder designed the world's first average female crash test dummy
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Scientists tagging 10,000 bogong moths to solve mystery
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Federal government tight-lipped on claims Australians were aboard US sub that sank Iranian warship
item.description: The federal government won't confirm if any Australian personnel were on a US fast-attack submarine that sank an Iranian warship, despite dozens of ADF members rotating through US submarines for training.

item.title: Lawsuit filed against Victoria Police over arrest related to Dezi Freeman search
item.description: A man arrested and held at gunpoint during the manhunt for alleged double murderer Dezi Freeman has filed a lawsuit against Victoria Police.

item.title: Scientists want help to solve mystery after tagging 10,000 bogong moths
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Iranian women's team sings national anthem after silence in opening game
item.description: Iran's female footballers stayed silent during the national anthem in their opening Women's Asian Cup game, but it was a different picture ahead of their game against Australia.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Hayley Raso's falconed, Mary Fowler and Amy Sayer sparkle — Matildas vs Iran quick hits
item.description: Hayley Raso cops an unfortunate double hit as Mary Fowler and Amy Sayer sparkle from the start on the Gold Coast. Here are the quick hits from the Matildas vs Iran at the Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: Live: Israel launches new wave of strikes on Beirut
item.description: The Israel military claims to be targeting "Hezbollah infrastructure" in the southern parts of the Lebanese capital — referred to as Dahiyeh. Follow live.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A shock radio departure, a promising study and a Canadian visit
item.description: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

item.title: Jimmy Barnes gives Virginia Trioli a music lesson and a few life lessons
item.description: Jimmy Barnes may be a rockstar with powerhouse vocals, but he's also a music history expert who loves gardening and cooking with his wife.

item.title: Women are often an 'afterthought' when it comes to designing products
item.description: Phones, cars, and the temperature in an office were all designed to suit the average man. Researchers say women are an afterthought in the way many products and spaces are designed.

item.title: 'Dangerous' Donald Trump chapter means 'nothing is safe' on global financial markets
item.description: A major market meltdown would change the financial plans of millions of Australians overnight. With the Middle East war, how likely is it?

item.title: Would Labor support this war from opposition?
item.description: When Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney addressed federal parliament on Thursday, his well-crafted speech had one gaping hole.

item.title: Nail salon workers voice concern over chemical exposures amid call for research in Australia
item.description: Walk-in nail salons are a mainstay of shopping hubs across Australia, but not enough is known about the health problems nail technicians face. Experts say it is time that changed.

item.title: NSW Health apologises to whistleblowing RPA hospital doctors over misconduct investigation
item.description: NSW Health apologises to three senior doctors at the Royal Prince Alfred Hospital after a two-year confidential investigation was found by the NSW Ombudsman to be unreasonable and unfair, in the latest example of dysfunction within two key departments.

item.title: Tasmanian Racing Minister Jane Howlett accrued $303,800 in legal fees, parliament discloses
item.description: Tasmanian Racing Minister Jane Howlett spent $303,800 of public money on legal fees "in the execution of her official duties", questioning in parliament reveals.

item.title: NSW government accused of 'stalling' on report into controversial gambling grants scheme
item.description: The ABC has learned the final report into the controversial ClubGRANTS scheme has been sitting on the state government's desk since January 2025.

item.title: SA election promise tracker 2026: What are Labor and the Liberals offering for you?
item.description: We've gone through the major parties' promises ahead of the state election so you can compare what they have announced for you.

item.title: Queensland town hopes dark sky certification attracts stargazers to the outback
item.description: The town of Winton receives international recognition after going "above and beyond" to protect its night sky.

item.title: Gold Coast mayor Tom Tate says ratepayers did not fund Trump family visit
item.description: ABC News can reveal more details about the Gold Coast mayor's visit to Mar-a-Lago before a Trump Tower deal was inked.

item.title: Britney Spears arrested on suspicion of drink driving
item.description: Pop star Britney Spears will face court in May after being arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence.

item.title: US 'accelerating' war in Iran as Israel threatens to 'bring hell' to Lebanon
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says the US is advancing its operation inside Iran, saying the "amount of firepower" over the country is about to "surge dramatically".

item.title: Polls close in Nepal's first election since deadly uprising forced out government
item.description: Helicopters will be used to collect ballot boxes from snowbound mountain regions across Nepal, home to eight of the world's 10 highest peaks, including Mount Everest.

item.title: Tropical low lashes Far North Queensland with up to 400mm of rain in parts
item.description: A tropical low has crossed the Queensland coast, bringing hundreds of millimetres of rain to some properties.

item.title: Australia names Ben Tudhope and Georgia Gunew as flag-bearers for Winter Paralympics opening ceremony
item.description: Ben Tudhope and Georgia Gunew will be Australia's flag-bearers at the opening ceremony for the Milano Cortina Games.

item.title: Inside Iran, some citizens are already preparing for 'freedom day'
item.description: Many Iranians feel like they are on the cusp of freedom after the US and Israel launched strikes on Saturday. But some also distrust the motives of both countries and are calling for an immediate end to the war.

item.title: Labor MP joins calls for Islamophobia to be added to Bondi royal commission
item.description: Labor backbencher Ed Husic says Islamophobia should be examined as part of Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: How the internet reacted to the Matildas' 4-0 win over Iran
item.description: The Matildas sealed a spot in the knockout stages of the AFC Women's Asian Cup with a 4-0 win over Iran, and the internet went wild.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: The Matildas embody Aussie pub rock spirit against Iran
item.description: The Joe Montemurro incarnation of the Matildas feels like a throwback to classic Australian rock. And in their Asian Cup win over Iran, they showed their mantra of playing free, but always going for the win, is starting to pay off.

item.title: Green whistles for IUD insertion among SA Labor's promises to support women's health
item.description: Women getting intra-uterine devices inserted in public clinics will be offered green whistles as pain relief as part of South Australian Labor's promise to take women's healthcare more seriously if re-elected.

item.title: Storm Hogan is a barrel racer
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Storm Hogan sets a new PB at Branxholme
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Bucking, ethics and OnlyFans: Australian rodeo in the social media age
item.description: Australian rodeos are caught in a high-stakes hustle to keep the outback alive in a country that some argue is outgrowing the buck.

item.title: Newcastle's Calvary Mater Hospital inquiry hears more mould claims
item.description: Families say mould issues at a Newcastle hospital date back more than 10 years.

item.title: India records seven-run win over England to advance to men's T20 World Cup final
item.description: India has survived a brilliant century from England's Jacob Bethell to record a seven-run win in a thrilling men's T20 World Cup semifinal at Mumbai's Wankhede Stadium.

item.title: Soul searching in northern NSW as $880 million Resilient Homes Program winds up
item.description: Communities say being the "guinea pig" of a flagship disaster recovery program has taken a toll.

item.title: $100b wiped off ASX this week as war shows no signs of easing — as it happened
item.description: The Australian share market fell sharply with more than $100 billion wiped off the boards this week. Look back on the day's financial news and insights from our specialist business reporters on our live blog.

item.title: Man wins seatbelt fine court battle as AI traffic cameras face scrutiny
item.description: A Queensland court decision casts fresh doubt on the reliability of controversial AI road-safety cameras after clearing a driver fined when his passenger moved their seatbelt during a trip.

item.title: Major flooding in Katherine as river 'rising rapidly'
item.description: Katherine residents went to sleep last night with predictions the town's river wouldn't even reach minor levels and woke up this morning to a major flood emergency with water "rising rapidly" this afternoon.

item.title: Remote residents struggling to afford food amid rising prices, despite grocery subsidy scheme
item.description: The head of Australia's biggest independent remote grocery stores network says in 30 years, he's never seen Indigenous communities struggle so much to afford enough food.

item.title: Mornington Island still in 'crisis' as residents claim taxpayer money being wasted
item.description: A wave of youth suicides lays bare a North Queensland community in crisis, with residents saying years of government investment have failed to create jobs, stability or hope.

item.title: Coroner finds Piper James died on K'gari from drowning due to dingo attack
item.description: A coronial investigation finds 19-year-old Canadian backpacker Piper James drowned due to a dingo attack on K'gari earlier this year.

item.title: Dementia-friendly choir in Busselton rekindles musical memories
item.description: Regional WA's only dementia-friendly singing group is helping people living with the degenerative condition to reconnect with their musical memories.

item.title: WA government not releasing report on countering violent extremism program
item.description: The WA government will not release an independent report on the effectiveness of its countering violent extremism program, almost two years after police fatally shot one of its radicalised teenage participants.

item.title: Contraceptive pill to be accessible for women in Victoria without prescription from July
item.description: Women getting the contraceptive pill for the first time will be able to access it from Victorian pharmacies without a prescription from July in a move that has "alarmed" GPs.

item.title: More women are involved in F1 than ever before. Yet there still hasn't been a female driver to compete in 50 years
item.description: As more women and girls flock to Formula 1, the sport now faces the challenge to convert that fandom into female participation.

item.title: Rock quarry proposal divides Hunter Valley community as demand for concrete soars
item.description: Industry groups say Australia needs to extract 200 million tonnes of rock material every year to support new housing and infrastructure projects, but quarry proposals often face community backlash.

item.title: Power bills surged when war broke out in 2022. Here's why the Middle East conflict may be different
item.description: The last time a global energy crisis sent Australian power bills skyrocketing was when Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022. What's changed this time?

item.title: Prisoner swap between Ukraine and Russia
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Ukraine, Russia free 200 POWs each in prisoner exchange
item.description: Ukraine and Russia each free 200 prisoners, the first part of an exchange that will see them swap 500 people each in total, officials say.

item.title: Teen photographer Casey Jacobs puts focus on life in rural WA
item.description: Narrogin local Casey Jacobs is on a mission to capture agricultural landscapes across the Great Southern and share the stories of life on the land.

item.title: Former senior ACT public servant calls for inquiry into treatment of First Nations staff
item.description: A former head of the Office of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Affairs in the ACT is calling for an inquiry to investigate the treatment and safety of First Nations staff in the territory's public service.

item.title: Evictions of homeless campers by City of Moreton Bay violated human rights, Supreme Court rules
item.description: The Supreme Court has found a south-east Queensland council violated the human rights of homeless campers.

item.title: Liberty Bell Bay faces shutdown after failure to lodge annual financial reports for five years
item.description: The corporate regulator moves to wind up the Liberty Bell Bay manganese smelter in northern Tasmania, citing the company's repeated failure to lodge financial statements.

item.title: Oil refinery in Bahrain on fire after Iranian strike
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Forever chemicals may threaten European honey bee colonies in Australia
item.description: The study also found PFOS exposure in European honey bee colonies led to the birth of smaller young and threatened the viability of their hives, researchers say.

item.title: Turon River in Sofala flows.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Rain fills Turon River in Sofala, NSW, after months of unstable water supply
item.description: Heavy rain across Central West NSW has brought a very welcome relief after a long few months without a drop of water in the Turon River in Sofala, 45 kilometres north of Bathurst.

item.title: Killer believed victim of Dimboola murder was a paedophile, court hears
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Jane Dixon is considering Thomas Clarke's sentence after he pleaded guilty to the brutal 2023 murder of a 72-year-old man.

item.title: Hillarys dog attack leaves girl in Perth Children's Hospital with serious facial injuries
item.description: A girl under the age of 10 is being treated in Perth Children's Hospital after being attacked by a dog while playing outside a home in Hillarys.

item.title: Chaos for Australian food exports as Middle East conflict disrupts transportation
item.description: The outbreak of fighting in the Middle East has left Australian businesses scrambling, with some products sitting in warehouses with nowhere to go.

item.title: Getting an ADHD diagnosis may become easier, but will it make things better?
item.description: The way ADHD is diagnosed and managed is changing across the country. Here's the reason for the change and how it will work in each state and territory.

item.title: Barrier Highway closed near Wilcannia by floodwaters with SES performing two rescues since yesterday
item.description: The Barrier Highway which connects Broken Hill to Dubbo and Sydney has been closed by major flooding with waters still expected to rise.

item.title: Australian actress Yerin Ha stars in new season of Bridgerton
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: F1 Australian Grand Prix: Oscar Piastri fastest in Friday practice, Aston Martin woes continue in Melbourne
item.description: Oscar Piastri has delighted his home fans by topping the time sheets today ahead of this weekend's Formula 1 season-opener in Melbourne.

item.title: Kurdish forces say they are ready to enter Iran, threatening new front in Middle East war
item.description: A coalition of Iranian Kurdish groups who oppose the regime say they are preparing to enter the war and have amassed thousands of fighters near the border with Iraq.

item.title: Justice Department publishes missing Epstein files involving uncorroborated claim about Trump
item.description: The Justice Department releases additional Jeffrey Epstein files involving uncorroborated accusations made by a woman against US President Donald Trump that the department said had been mistakenly withheld during an earlier review.

item.title: What to watch, read and listen to this weekend: Lost Lambs, Help(2) and Ali's Wedding
item.description: From a laugh-out-loud novel to classic TV for adults and kids, there's plenty of culture to dig into this weekend.

item.title: Kristi Noem could be just the first cabinet secretary in Donald Trump's firing line
item.description: Kristi Noem discovers that loyalty alone doesn't make you untouchable, becoming the first cabinet secretary to be fired in Donald Trump's second administration.

item.title: Oil's muted reaction to Trump's attack on Iran
item.description: For all the chaos, the terror and the loss of life in the past week following America's war on Iran, oil prices have managed to maintain a semblance of stability.

item.title: 'Suicidal' Hezbollah faces reckoning in Lebanon after joining Middle East war
item.description: Finding criticism of the Islamist militant group and political party — an organisation once seemingly untouchable — is much easier than it used to be on the streets of Beirut.

item.title: Two 'beautiful girls' remembered as 25yo man jailed for causing fatal crash
item.description: A District Court Judge has jailed a 25-year-old who "deliberately" ignored a stop sign and give way sign before colliding with a bus, killing his two passengers.

item.title: Flood damage devastates Alice Springs drug and alcohol rehab site Aranda House
item.description: A therapeutic rehabilitation service may have to find a new home after a severe flood in Alice Springs left its rooms unusable.

item.title: Donald Trump hints Cuba deal may be his next focus after war in Iran
item.description: Almost a week into the war being waged by the US and Israel on Iran, Donald Trump hints that Cuba could become his next focus.

item.title: Former Canberra Reptile Zoo employee jailed for sexually abusing, grooming teenage girls
item.description: A former employee at the Canberra Reptile Zoo is sentenced to more than five years' jail after being found guilty of grooming a girl under 16, and sexually assaulting a girl under 18, that he met through their shared interest in reptiles.

item.title: Families aboard Middle East flights criticise DFAT's Iran response
item.description: Flights from Middle Eastern travel hubs are arriving in Australia half-empty despite thousands of Australians remaining stranded in the region.

item.title: Live: Sutherland on fire as Australia takes charge on day one against India
item.description: Australia is out to seal a multi-format series victory over India as the sides meet for the sole Test match, in what will also be Alyssa Healy's final game for Australia. Follow live.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Fears for Iran's women's football team after state TV labels players 'traitors'
item.description: There are concerns for the safety of Iran's female footballers after they were condemned on Iranian state TV as wartime traitors for not singing the national anthem before their first game.

item.title: Man charged with murder after death of 69-year-old at Burpengary property
item.description: A 35-year-old man is due to face court in Brisbane tomorrow over the alleged murder.

item.title: Vehicle seized as renewed search for missing SA woman Trisha Graf concludes
item.description: A renewed search for Trisha Graf, 41, in the remote SA outback has wound up and a vehicle of interest seized at Andamooka will be forensically examined.

item.title: F1 Australian Grand Prix: Aston Martin and Honda issues continue at Albert Park
item.description: Aston Martin is running the risk of not being able to participate in Sunday's Australian Grand Prix, at least, as issues grow for the beleaguered team.

item.title: Gagebrook teenager wins back Friday bus service after two years of walking through swamp to school
item.description: Changes to bus services meant Jack Triffitt's had to walk to school every Friday, traversing across a swamp, multiple hills, a dirt road and a bridge. So he lobbied politicians, government departments and Metro for change.

item.title: SES women share experiences of sexism, inappropriate behaviour in Queensland as previously blocked report released
item.description: A review into SES member experiences hears allegations of sexual harassment, stalking and pockets of "boys' clubs" operating throughout the state.

item.title: Daintree river businesses lose boats
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Family of Indigenous man who died in custody say justice 'won't be done' as NSW coroner hands down findings
item.description: The inquest heard that Greg Merriman had a heart attack and died alone in his locked cell after tear gas was deployed by Corrective Services officers in his prison wing.

item.title: Live: Broncos crash back to earth as Panthers get revenge in NRL opener
item.description: The Panthers remind the NRL of their brilliance with a ruthless 26-0 win over premiers Brisbane to kick off their 2026 season, exacting revenge for last year's epic preliminary final loss at Lang Park. Follow live.

item.title: Albany murder investigation turns into double homicide case as injured son dies
item.description: Police say they are treating the death of a 69-year-old grandmother as a double homicide investigation, after her son, who was also found at the scene with serious injuries, died.

item.title: OAM recipient Geoff Guest sentenced to 12 years' jail for child sexual abuse
item.description: A man details in court how his life was affected by the sexual abuse perpetrated against him by a World War II veteran and Order of Australia Medal recipient, now aged 99.

item.title: ADF says helicopter forced to take evasive action during 'unsafe interaction' with Chinese military
item.description: The Australian Defence Force says a Seahawk helicopter launched from the Australian frigate HMAS Toowoomba was confronted by a Chinese helicopter and was forced to "take evasion to maintain a safe flight".

item.title: Labor flips against Lee Point development ahead of Nightcliff by-election
item.description: During its eight years in government, Territory Labor supported an 800-home defence development in Darwin, but just before the Nightcliff by-election its candidate reveals the party's position has changed.

item.title: Collingullie Wagga Demons Aussie Rules official steps down over sexist comments
item.description: A southern NSW football club says an official has stepped down after making derogatory comments about players on its women's team.

item.title: West Melbourne mosque closed to public while police investigate suspicious package
item.description: A mosque in West Melbourne has temporarily closed while police investigate reports of a suspicious package.

item.title: Sculpture by the Sea exhibition returns to Perth's Cottesloe Beach after funding battle
item.description: The annual Sculpture by the Sea art exhibition returns to Cottesloe Beach in Perth after a funding shortfall forced its cancellation last year.

item.title: Jackie O insists she did not quit hit radio show she hosted with Kyle Sandilands
item.description: Jackie "O" Henderson has denied she quit her hit KIIS FM radio show she hosted with Kyle Sandilands, saying she was left in "shock" by the news.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Plucky Bangladesh teases much-needed jeopardy, North Korea and China are quarterfinal bound
item.description: For 45 minutes, minnows Bangladesh pushed North Korea all the way. It was a breath of fresh air for a tournament desperately lacking close matches.

item.title: Katherine flooding drone vision
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Friday finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Live: Suns making red-hot start to AFL season against Geelong
item.description: Gold Coast have big wraps after last year's breakthrough finals campaign, but they face a stern test to kick off their season against grand finalists Geelong at Carrara. Follow live.

item.title: WA woman who poked holes in housemate's condoms found not guilty of intent to cause harm
item.description: A Bunbury woman has been found not guilty of intent to cause harm after she admitted to tampering with her housemate's condoms while she was in a "fit of rage".

item.title: Judge orders Sanjeev Gupta's Tahmoor mine be wound up with liquidators appointed
item.description: A Supreme Court judge approves a winding-up order for the embattled Tahmoor mine, owned by billionaire Sanjeev Gupta.

item.title: Adelaide man spent his final moments ramped outside hospital, partner says
item.description: An Adelaide man spent some of his final moments stuck in an ambulance outside a major Adelaide public hospital, his partner says.

item.title: Police investigating death of man in Mernda following reports of fight
item.description: Police are investigating a man's death in Melbourne's north this evening after reports of a group of men fighting.

item.title: Surf lifesaver missing off Buddina Beach on Sunshine Coast found dead
item.description: The alarm was raised when the 18-year-old failed to return to the beach on Wednesday, setting off a major search operation.
